notes: IETF publishes RFCs (Request for Comments) that define internet protocols and standards. RFC identifiers are numeric.

data.description: Atom Publishing Protocol extension for sharing structured security information (vulnerability descriptions, software identifiers, configuration checklists) via web feeds. Defined by IETF RFC 8322.
data.notes: ROLIE is a publishing/transport format — it specifies how to structure security-data feeds, not how to interpret or act on their content. Placed under reference. Also accessible via secid:reference/ietf.org/8322 (numeric RFC pattern).

data.description: IETF RFC identified by numeric sequence number.
data.notes: RFCs define internet protocols, standards, and best practices. Published by the RFC Editor. Once published, an RFC number is never reused or changed. RFCs may be obsoleted or updated by newer RFCs.
